headline: Eli Lilly Faces Actos RICO Class Action And Investor Cash Flow Questions
summary: A US court has certified a nationwide third party payor class in a lawsuit involving Eli Lilly’s former diabetes drug Actos. The case alleges concealment of Actos’ bladder cancer risk and claims violations of the Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act. Class certification expands the potential claimant pool to thousands of payors and raises the prospect of material financial and reputational impact for NYSE:LLY. Eli Lilly, trading on the NYSE under the ticker LLY, now faces this...

headline: Fauna Bio Announces Target Designation Milestone in Obesity Discovery Collaboration
summary: EMERYVILLE, Calif., March 19, 2026--Fauna Bio, a biotechnology company pioneering the use of extreme mammal biology and comparative genomics to discover novel therapeutic targets, today announced the designation of a target under its strategic obesity discovery collaboration with Eli Lilly and Company (Lilly), initiated in December, 2023.

content.title: Fauna Bio Announces Target Designation Milestone in Obesity Discovery Collaboration
content.summary: EMERYVILLE, Calif., March 19, 2026--Fauna Bio, a biotechnology company pioneering the use of extreme mammal biology and comparative genomics to discover novel therapeutic targets, today announced the designation of a target under its strategic obesity discovery collaboration with Eli Lilly and Company (Lilly), initiated in December, 2023.

headline: Lilly's triple agonist, retatrutide, demonstrated significant reductions in A1C and weight in first Phase 3 trial for treatment of type 2 diabetes
summary: Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) today announced positive topline results from TRANSCEND-T2D-1, a Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating the efficacy and safety of retatrutide, an investigational first-in-class GIP, GLP-1 and glucagon triple hormone receptor agonist, as an adjunct to diet and exercise. The trial enrolled adults diagnosed with type 2 diabetes with inadequate glycemic control with diet and exercise alone, and a mean duration of diabetes of 2.5 years. In the study, retatrutide met the
